mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with logistic accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side stress that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong underlying economic momentum that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 on 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >58% of observations when CPIAUCSL remains above 305.0.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates labor market deterioration masked by participation effects, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative predictive accuracy of <50% over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity that boosts near-term earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to generate positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances.
